reviews.review_msg: Her English isn't great and the lectures were confusing. Tutorial was even worse. I found her notes to be disorganized as well. The textbook is ok, but more detailed than what you need to know. Assignments are best way to learn the material and similar questions will be on the exams. She explains things better in person, go to her office hours.
She is responsible and helpful for students. Exams were not hard. All the thing you need is understand her notes and do assignments your own. Textbook is not necessary.
long lecture, confusing teaching style, lots of assignment, hard exam. she will not make you have better understanding of what you should learn, the only words she will tell you is practice more.
She is nice actually. This course includes lots of materials. Her exams are  a little bit tricky but generally it is not hard.

take this prof for 330 if u get the chance. she gives many opportunities for you to boost ur mark if u did poorly in exams. the class did poorly on 2nd midterm so she shifted the weight away to 1st midterm where everybody did well. she also provides a bomb ass cheat sheet for exams.  michelle is so based. god bless.
The lecture was boring!! Exams are open book. Midterm is easy, but the final is damn hard!! The assignments are marked really low!!

She used a lot of Richard Lockhart's stuff so I would probably take it with him. Textbook was much more helpful than being in class, TAs in the STAT room well it depends which one you get and then its a 50% chance they are helpful. Midterm too easy final too hard too many questions and even though it was open book you really had to know your stuff!
It was hard to understand her during the lecture; and the textbook was much more useful than the her note. Would not recommend this prof!
The assignments were fair, and the first midterm was easy, however the final was extremely hard and class average dropped; overall a decent but not great professor

Ask questions by email instead of in class- she is way nicer on email! Midterm was almost identical to practise midterm. Final was "all notes are allowed" and combined concepts that we had at least touched on in class. On the final, I mostly used the assignment solutions, practise final solutions, and the "further topics" slides, and I got an A.

She's a very nice lady but she cannot communicate the material. Her midterm and final were both open book. She made the midterm so easy that half the class got 100%... so she HAD to make the final fairly difficult to "balance" things out. Very unfair exams. The assignments were okay but most of the TAs weren't helpful at all so don't expect much.

Tough class, especially if unable to teach yourself concepts.  Open book midterm and final make things easier, but there is a catch.  The midterm and first few assignments are really easy, but the assignments after the midterm are marked very hard, and the final was ridiculous.  Hardly any of here questions were ones we had even previously. AVOID!

When it comes to learning the material, rely on your textbook and not her. Open book midterm/final. I had a tough time sitting through her lectures, which are completely useless anyways. Slides are missing crucial information and she is constantly correcting mistakes. Would never recommend this prof to anyone, especially if stats isn't your forte.

Open book midterm/final. Fairly nice prof, just a little hard to understand. The comment below saying those comments were from closed minded individuals is not necessarily true. The prof did refuse to explain a concept she had discussed 'previously (from last lecture)'. And it is true that you will probably learn more from the book than her.

She is not very clear at all but her exams and assignments are fairly easy. Her notes won't help unless you go to class. Took me an entire semester to learn what the word "arier" means. But extremely nice lady!

She loves stats, and is keen on teaching/has a good attitude, but she is not clear at all.  Anything I have learned has been from my own studying of the textbook.  She makes concepts convoluted, not concise at all.  Open book midterm/final, but you better go get some outside help to understand what is going on... Avoid unless you can teach yourself

Michelle is a fairly good STATS professor. Her notes are very organized. She keeps class on schedule and is always available for help. She does have an accent, which bothers some folks. My suggestion is to attend lecture and gain some information, then review that chapter in the book. Most comments here are probably from close minded individuals.

She's very nice and cute person but her accent is a little hard for me to understand. It was hard to keep up with what she was trying to explain. I ended up reading the textbook instead of listening to her. The midterm was open book though, so that's a plus!

She's very nice lady but awful prof. Her accent is so thick its absolutely impossible to follow what shes saying. When asked questions in class she responds with "Already explained the concept in previous lectures" and refuses to answer your question. Midterm wasnt that hard but assignments are very hrd. Awful prof.

She is the most disorganized professor I have ever had, especially the lecture slides. A lot of the questions on the assignments are the same as those of the 201 course, which is unfair under all circumstances and there is no excuse for this.

She is a nice prof but she isn't clear in her explanations. She frequently revises her notes after using them in lecture. Her assignments are really long and are mostly textbook questions.You will be learning more from the textbook than from her
Not good at explaining the concepts, most of the slides are filled with useless information. No enjoyment at all throughout this class.

She is good at statistics, and she has tried her best to release as much office hour as she can. you don't have to use textbook, her note is really clarity. I do suggest you to take her class, and visit her office hour if you need.
